record.text: Autodesk is building an agentic AI Assistant using Amazon Bedrock and deploying ML insights on Amazon SageMaker, with AWS services like EKS, DynamoDB, and OpenSearch. Goal: cut design workflow manual effort from hours to minutes. #AWS #Bedrock #SageMaker #GenAI

external.description: Autodesk is on a mission to empower everyone, everywhere to create solutions to challenges big and small. The company produces software for a range of Design and Make industries including architecture, construction, engineering, manufacturing, and media and entertainment. To better support more than 100 million customers worldwide, Autodesk migrated workloads from its on-premises data centers to Amazon Web Services (AWS), taking a hybrid cloud approach. As a result, Autodesk has increased reliability, scalability, and security, while saving up to 90 percent on data processing costs. Freed from manual processes, Autodesk’s data scientists can now focus on further innovation, using big data, machine learning, and serverless solutions on AWS.
external.title: Autodesk Innovators

National Frog Jumping Day 🐸🏆

images.alt:
National Frog Jumping Day is celebrated on May 13th. It is a day that honors the tradition of frog jumping, which has been popular in many parts of the country since the late 1800s.
The origins of frog jumping can be traced back to a famous story by Mark Twain called
"The Celebrated Jumping Frog of Calaveras County," which was first published in 1865.
The story tells of a man named Jim Smiley who makes a bet that his frog, Dan' Webster, can jump farther than any other frog in the county.
Since then, frog jumping has become a popular pastime and competitive sport in many parts of the United States, particularly in California. Each year, a variety of frog jumping contests and events are held, where participants compete to see whose frog can jump the farthest distance. 
National Frog Jumping Day is a fun and lighthearted way to celebrate this quirky and unique aspect of American culture. It is a day to appreciate the fun and entertainment that frog jumping provides, and to celebrate the spirit of competition and camaraderie that it fosters.

images.alt: A tall, narrow industrial studio stretches upward like a converted warehouse cathedral, flooded with pale natural daylight from massive vertical windows in the background. Suspended from the ceiling are dozens of hanging design fragments, loose fabrics, pinned sketches, architectural renderings, unfinished garments, and translucent concept sheets that sway gently through the upper half of the frame like floating memories. In the center foreground stands an older man with a shaved head and sharp facial features, dressed entirely in layered black clothing. His long tailored coat falls almost to his knees, textured and structured like avant-garde couture. His posture is calm and composed, hands partially tucked into his coat pockets, staring directly toward the viewer with an expression that feels analytical, emotionally distant, and deeply intentional. Around him, blurred assistants and mannequins occupy the studio floor, creating the impression of an active creative workshop frozen in soft motion. Warm practical lamps glow faintly near the sides while the rest of the room remains washed in cool gray daylight. The composition emphasizes verticality and isolation, with hanging papers and fabric forming a tunnel-like corridor that frames the designer as the still center of a chaotic creative environment. The atmosphere feels quiet, intellectual, and slightly surreal, like stepping inside the mind of someone redesigning culture itself.

images.alt:
A tiny handmade creature stands atop a stack of cracked stones in the middle of a dusty, sunlit landscape. The little figure appears to be crafted from a worn pebble or seed pod, with stitched black button-like eyes, tiny curved arms, and rough twine strands sprouting from the top of its head like messy hair. A small strip of fabric or bandage crosses diagonally over one side of its face, giving it the appearance of a battered survivor.
The creature balances carefully on a pile of rounded gray stones, the largest stone below it split deeply down the center with jagged red mineral veins exposed through the cracks. The surrounding ground is covered in grit, scattered acorn shells, dried plant fragments, and glowing flecks of dust suspended in warm afternoon light.
The background is heavily blurred with creamy bokeh and soft golden highlights, creating the feeling of an enormous world towering beyond the tiny subject. Despite its fragile appearance, the creature stands upright with determination, isolated but resilient in a harsh environment. The shallow depth of field and low-angle macro perspective make the miniature figure feel strangely heroic, as though it has climbed to the peak of a ruined stone monument after surviving some unseen catastrophe.
Keywords:
macro photography, tiny creature, pebble character, cracked stone, whimsical horror, shallow depth of field, miniature world, dusty landscape, stitched face, survivor aesthetic, warm bokeh, handmade being

images.alt:
A towering gothic cathedral dominates the center of the image, its impossibly tall spire disappearing into a sky split between storm clouds and radiant white light. The structure appears partially physical and partially spectral, as though carved from stone, ice, and refracted glass simultaneously. Massive vertical lines pull the eye upward while shimmering fragments of iridescent color scatter across the cathedral’s surface like digital static trapped inside stained crystal.
Dark fog and smoke choke the lower half of the scene, obscuring much of the foundation and giving the building the appearance of emerging from another dimension. The cathedral’s architecture features pointed arches, narrow lancet windows, clustered towers, and layered buttress-like forms that blend into haze and collapsing light. Small bursts of warm orange illumination glow from within lower openings, creating sharp contrast against the cold blue-gray atmosphere.
At the bottom foreground, a crowd of ghostly pale figures stretches upward toward the cathedral. Their forms are blurred and almost melted together, with thin raised arms reaching through the mist as though pleading, worshipping, or being drawn toward the luminous structure. The surrounding air feels turbulent and charged, filled with drifting particles, vapor, and streaks of fractured light that make the entire scene appear unstable and transcendent at once.
Keywords:
gothic cathedral, ethereal spire, storm clouds, spectral crowd, fractured light, dreamscape, atmospheric fog, sacred architecture, dark fantasy, luminous haze
AI-generated; use is permitted for non-commercial creative projects. MidJourney, FLUX.1-schnell model via Perchance, Artspace.ai, REVE, Echoform™.

images.alt:
A hyper-detailed dark cybernetic wolf head emerging from shadow, centered composition, facing directly toward the viewer with an aggressive snarling expression. The wolf is constructed from layered black chrome, gunmetal steel, and biomechanical armor plates fused with organic fur textures. Sharp metallic spikes and jagged blade-like fur protrude outward in chaotic asymmetrical patterns. Thin glowing red energy wires and vein-like cables wrap across the face and mane, creating a sinister technological appearance.
Its eyes glow with intense crimson neon light, piercing through the darkness with an ominous predatory stare. The ears feature translucent red illuminated inner structures, while fractured mechanical plating across the forehead resembles a futuristic battle mask. Exposed fangs and metallic teeth add to the savage ferocity.
The background is a dark industrial wall with subtle texture, fading into smoky shadows and atmospheric haze. Dramatic cinematic lighting with deep blacks, glossy reflections, red rim light, and high contrast enhances the terrifying cyberpunk aesthetic. Ultra-realistic rendering, sharp focus, intricate textures, volumetric shadows, dark sci-fi horror atmosphere, premium concept art quality, unreal engine render, octane render.
